title: Incorrect `$pow` transform by opt_expr
body:
### Version

v0.61

### On which OS did this happen?

macOS

### Reproduction Steps

```
read_rtlil <<EOF
module \top
  wire width 3 input 2 \binary

  wire width 32 output 3 \y

  cell $pow $0
    parameter \A_WIDTH 32
    parameter \B_WIDTH 3
    parameter \A_SIGNED 1
    parameter \B_SIGNED 0
    parameter \Y_WIDTH 32
    connect \A 2
    connect \B \binary
    connect \Y \y
  end
end
EOF

dump
opt_expr
dump
```

### Expected Behavior

The transformed netlist is equivalent to the original

### Actual Behavior

```
  cell $pow $0
    parameter \A_WIDTH 32
    parameter \B_WIDTH 3
    parameter \A_SIGNED 1
    parameter \B_SIGNED 0
    parameter \Y_WIDTH 32
    connect \A 2
    connect \B \binary
    connect \Y \y
  end
```
is replaced with
```
  cell $shl $0
    parameter \A_WIDTH 1
    parameter \B_WIDTH 3
    parameter \A_SIGNED 1
    parameter \B_SIGNED 0
    parameter \Y_WIDTH 32
    connect \A 1'1
    connect \B \binary
    connect \Y \y
  end
```

Note the A input of the new instance is tied to `-1`. The function of the two netlists differs, take e.g.
```
eval -set binary 0
```
which first gives the result
```
Eval result: \y = 1.
```
and then
```
Eval result: \y = 32'11111111111111111111111111111111.
```
code_blocks:
read_rtlil <<EOF
module \top
  wire width 3 input 2 \binary

  wire width 32 output 3 \y

  cell $pow $0
    parameter \A_WIDTH 32
    parameter \B_WIDTH 3
    parameter \A_SIGNED 1
    parameter \B_SIGNED 0
    parameter \Y_WIDTH 32
    connect \A 2
    connect \B \binary
    connect \Y \y
  end
end
EOF

dump
opt_expr
dump

cell $pow $0
    parameter \A_WIDTH 32
    parameter \B_WIDTH 3
    parameter \A_SIGNED 1
    parameter \B_SIGNED 0
    parameter \Y_WIDTH 32
    connect \A 2
    connect \B \binary
    connect \Y \y
  end

cell $shl $0
    parameter \A_WIDTH 1
    parameter \B_WIDTH 3
    parameter \A_SIGNED 1
    parameter \B_SIGNED 0
    parameter \Y_WIDTH 32
    connect \A 1'1
    connect \B \binary
    connect \Y \y
  end
